purpose: Per-claim argument certainty calibration (overclaim profile): for ONE argument-shaped document, profile — per load-bearing claim — whether the claim's EXPRESSED certainty (hedged ↔ assertive) matches the EVIDENTIAL support it actually carries, flagging overclaim (asserted hard / thin support) and underclaim (tentative / strong support). The per-claim certainty×support→alignment table IS the read — NEVER a single "overconfidence score", NEVER a verdict that the author is arrogant / sloppy / dishonest. Claim extraction + per-claim support are one NEW extract_claims LLM-judge pass (argument_judge labels PARAGRAPHS, not claims); expressed certainty is the DETERMINISTIC frozen-lexicon substrate (HEDGE_VOCAB / BOOSTER_VOCAB, multi-word or word-boundary-guarded, no bare "may") computed over each claim's quote. The legitimate-strong-claim filter ships ONLY the two EVIDENCE-GATED defenses (defended_stipulated: a str.find-validated stipulation marker in the claim; defended_elsewhere: a REAL in-document supporting locus validated text[start:end]==quote — a fabricated cross-ref is a build error); the judgmental defenses (defended_analytic / defended_common_ground) are M2-only and never fire in M1. The no-verdict firewall is MECHANICAL (a CERTAINTY-SCOPED rename of within_doc_segmentation's assert_no_authorship: FORBIDDEN_RESULT_KEYS + FORBIDDEN_SUBSTRINGS + a recursive assert_no_verdict guard → policy_refused). The PER-CLAIM complement to stance_modality_audit's DOCUMENT-LEVEL hedge/booster distribution. M1 = mock-deterministic (CI-safe); M2 = anthropic (lazy/fail-loud). Ships heuristic — directional, no numeric anchor. Single-document scope.
use_when: You have ONE argument-shaped document (op-ed / brief / testimony / academic argument, >= ~50 words) and want a per-claim read of where expressed certainty outruns (overclaim) or undersells (underclaim) the evidence the claim carries
You want a claim-LOCALIZED certainty↔support mismatch that a document-level hedge/booster distribution (stance_modality_audit) cannot produce
You want the legitimate-strong-claim defenses shown explicitly (a stipulated or elsewhere-supported strong claim is marked defended_*, not flagged as overclaim)
do_not_use_when: You want a verdict that the author is overconfident / arrogant / a Dunning-Kruger case / dishonest / sloppy, or that the argument is unsound — the surface refuses all of these (high certainty is NOT arrogance, low certainty is NOT weakness); there is no overconfidence or calibration score
You want a DOCUMENT-LEVEL stance/modality distribution (how much the prose hedges overall) — that is stance_modality_audit's job; this surface is per-claim
You want to know whether a claim is TRUE — this measures certainty↔support alignment, not truth, and never supplies evidence or rewrites the claim
You don't have an LLM judge or a pre-computed claim manifest

inputs.target: a positional path or --target (single file; required)
inputs.judge: --judge mock (M1, deterministic CI) | manifest | anthropic (M2, lazy/fail-loud) with --judge-model / --judge-manifest
inputs.support_loci: --support-loci (optional JSON sidecar of candidate defended_elsewhere supporting loci keyed by topic_ref; validated against the document — a fabricated locus is a build error)

compute.cost_note: M1 (--judge mock) is pure-stdlib, deterministic, CI-runnable (no API, no models): the certainty lexicon and the legitimate-strong-claim filter are mechanical Python; only claim extraction + support are judged, and the mock judge is marker-driven. M2 (--judge anthropic, $0.01–0.10 per doc) is the real extraction lens (lazy SDK import, fail-loud).

examples.description: Single-doc M1 calibration with the deterministic mock judge
M2 spot-check with a real API judge
